evidences.title: MegaETH Publishes Specifics for Upcoming Public Token Sale
evidences.content: Auction Launch: MegaETH MegaETH will auction 500M MEGA tokens (5% of total supply) in a 72-hour English auction that begins on October 27. The sale will have a starting price of $0.0001 per token ($1M FDV) and feature a ceiling price of $0.0999 per token ($999M FDV). Auction Mechanics: The sale will run on Sonar, Coinbase’s recently acquired crypto crowdfunding platform. Participants can bid using USDT on Ethereum, with individual contributions capped to $186,282 maximum. The auction format allows the price to rise dynamically based on demand, with allocations finalized once bidding closes after 72 hours. evidences.title: MegaETH unveils public sale details as traders price the token at a ...
evidences.content: Share Photo: MegaETH MegaETH has unveiled details of its public sale, offering 5% of its token supply through an English auction on Sonar by Echo. The sale starts at a $1 million fully diluted valuation (FDV) and is capped at $999 million. Running from October 27 to 30, the auction allows bids between $2,650 and $186,282 in USDT. Participants can choose a one-year lockup for a 10% discount, mandatory for accredited U.S. investors and optional for others. Described as the “first real-time blockchain,” MegaETH aims to build scalable infrastructure on Ethereum. A previous Echo sale co-hosted by Cobie drew over 3,000 investors, including Dragonfly, Joseph Lubin, and Vitalik Buterin. [...] Share MegaETH has unveiled details of its public sale, offering 5% of its token supply through an English auction on Sonar by Echo. evidences.title: MegaETH Public Sale Launches on Sonar with Limited Access
evidences.content:  Futures menu tag  Spot  Onchain  Earn  More  menu logo Rewards Hub DownloadEnglish # MegaETH Public Sale Launches on Sonar with Limited Access Share MegaETH is set to launch its public sale on the Sonar platform at 21:00 UTC+8 on October 27. The sale will offer 500 million tokens out of a total supply of 10 billion, with payments accepted in USDT. Bids can range from $2,650 to $186,282, starting at a price of $0.0001 and capped at $0.0999. An analysis by a trader on X highlights that the MegaETH sale is highly selective, with only about 1 in 50 applicants expected to receive an allocation. This approach mirrors previous Echo projects, which have been known for high returns but limited access, suggesting that profits may be reserved for a small group of participants. evidences.title: MegaETH ICO Crosses $1 Billion in Commitments - "The Defiant"
evidences.content: Advertisement # MegaETH ICO Crosses $1 Billion in Commitments MegaETH ICO Crosses $1 Billion in Commitments MegaETH, a highly anticipated ‘real-time’ Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain, is set to wrap up its initial coin offering (ICO) at 9 am EST on Oct. 30, and despite the raise’s $50 million cap, more than $1 billion is competing for a MEGA token allocation. According to a community-run dashboard, nearly 38,000 users have contributed to the sale with an average bid of around $25,000. the-defiant The ICO is being conducted via Sonar and is MegaETH’s third community-led raise, following an Echo round in 2024 and a soulbound NFT collection, dubbed the Fluffles, that dropped in February. [...] Participants can bid up to $186,282 at a valuation of their choice, up to a maximum fully diluted valuation (FDV) of $999 million. While the average valuation of each bid is unknown, it is safe to assume that the full $50 million will be sold at the maximum valuation, considering the demand. The strong demand is partially catalyzed by the MEGA token’s strong pre-market performance. The token’s pre-market perpetuals on Hyperliquid are trading at $0.43, or a $4.3 billion FDV, which would give ICO co...

evidences.title: MegaETH Prepares for Sonar Token Sale and Public Launch
evidences.content: It began on June 27, 2024, with a $20 million seed round led by Dragonfly Capital. This wasn’t your typical anonymous VC roster. Among the backers were Vitalik Buterin, Joseph Lubin of ConsenSys, and Sreeram Kannan, the mind behind EigenLayer. That mix alone signaled how deeply MegaETH was plugged into Ethereum’s inner circle. The round closed at a $100 million post-money valuation, setting a confident tone for what came next. evidences.title: MegaETH Public Sale Launches Tonight on Sonar - KuCoin
evidences.content: As reported by PANews, MegaETH will launch its public sale at 21:00 UTC+8 on October 27 on Sonar, a platform under Echo. The total supply is 10 billion tokens, with 500 million available for public auction, payable in USDT. The auction will allow bids between $2,650 and $186,282, with a starting price of $0.0001 (FDV $1 million) and a maximum cap of $0.0999 (FDV $999 million). An analysis by an encrypted trader on X suggests that while previous Echo projects like $XPL offered high returns, the $MEGA sale is more selective, with only about 1 in 50 applicants likely to receive an allocation. The trader warns that the sale is designed to filter out most participants, with profits likely reserved for a small, prioritized group. ## GemSlot ### Complete Tasks to Earn Free Tokens Every Day [...] ### Complete Tasks to Earn Free Tokens Every Day ## Limited-time offer for newcomers! ### Newcomer Bonus: Up to USDT in Rewards! evidences.title: MegaETH token sale oversubscribed by 8.9x with $450M committed
evidences.content: Get started CointelegraphCointelegraph # MegaETH token sale oversubscribed by 8.9x with $450M committed 2 min read ETHUSDT Ethereum layer-2 network MegaETH’s initial coin offering reportedly “sold out” within minutes and is now oversubscribed by about $400 million, as users scramble to get an allocation of its MEGA token. The Ethereum layer-2 network opened the auction for its MEGA token on Monday with a raise cap of just under $50 million, but the auction has since seen more than $450 million in commitments in just a matter of hours. In total, the initial coin offering (ICO) will allocate 5% of the total 10 billion token supply, with a maximum bid amount of $186,282 and a minimum bid of $2,650, offering the option to select a one-year lock-up for a 10% discount. [...] Because it’s oversubscribed, when the countdown timer ends in two days, a “special allocation mechanism” will be used to decide each user’s allocation, according to the auction FAQ, which takes into account past engagement in the MegaETH and Ethereum communities, and whether a lock-up was selected. The project’s white paper lists the token launch date as January 2026. The token will be tradeable on specific centra...

evidences.title: MegaETH | Public Sale
evidences.content: MEGA PUBLIC SALE USER MANUAL. 10,000,000,000 tokens. Token supply reserved for this public sale. 500,000,000 tokens (5% of total supply). English Auction with a ceiling price. $0.0001 ($1 million FDV). $0.0999 ($999 million FDV). U.S. accredited investors have a mandatory 1-year lock-up with a 10% discount. The lock-up is optional for non-U.S. participants. By clicking "Accept", you agree to the storing of cookies on your device to enhance site navigation, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts. View our Cookies Policy for more information. Identity verification via Sonar is required for anyone who intends to participate in the potential offering. Information such as social media accounts, GitHub account and additional wallets is optional but may increase your chance of receiving allocation in the case of oversubscription. Profile locked when the sale ended. To start, please link ONE wallet you want to use to place bids in the potential sale on Sonar. Using a fresh wallet does not hurt your eligibility to participate. On this website, connect the same wallet linked on Sonar.

evidences.title: MegaETH raises the USDm limit to 1 billion USD
evidences.content: / RootData search FundraisingMarket Fundraising Projects Calendar Token Unlocks Investors List Crypto Stocks People Deals Ecosystem Tags Archives ROOTDATA LIST X Data News Sign in # MegaETH raises the USDm limit to 1 billion USD Nov 25, 2025 23:19:29 Share to MegaETH announced that the $250 million cap was filled in 156 seconds. They decided to raise the cap to $1 billion so that users can obtain USDm on the first day. The bridge will reopen at 11 AM Eastern Time. The purpose of this event is to distribute USDm to a more diverse group of users. This will not affect previous participants. All MegaETH contracts remain secure and have been audited by Zellic and Slowmist. No State updates were performed for 3h 7m (from 2026 Mar 03, 07:55 UTC until 2026 Mar 03, 11:02 UTC). These typically occur every 59min 59s on average. [...] Proving any of the 3600 intermediate state commitments in a proposal faulty invalidates the entire proposal. Proving a proposal valid invalidates all conflicting siblings. Pruning of a tournament’s children happens strictly chronologically, which guarantees that the first faulty proposal of a given proposer is always pruned first.
